subpath_example: from os.path import join → os (built-in, ignore)
builtin_modules: os, sys, re, json, math, time, datetime, pathlib, typing, collections, itertools, functools, io, abc, copy, enum, dataclasses, contextlib, logging, warnings, hashlib, base64, struct, socket, threading, multiprocessing, subprocess, shutil, tempfile, glob, fnmatch, configparser, argparse, unittest, ast, inspect, importlib, gc, weakref, string, textwrap, pprint, traceback
language_rules: 'import pkg' and 'from pkg import x' both count as usage
Side-effect imports count as usage: `import pkg` with no explicit use (e.g., monkey-patching, registering plugins)

Package name may differ from import name — apply mapping:
  PIL → pillow
  cv2 → opencv-python
  sklearn → scikit-learn
  bs4 → beautifulsoup4
  yaml → pyyaml
  dotenv → python-dotenv
  Crypto → pycryptodome
  usaddress → usaddress
  magic → python-magic
  gi → PyGObject
  wx → wxPython
  gtk → PyGTK
  apt → python-apt
  serial → pyserial
  MySQLdb → mysqlclient
  psycopg2 → psycopg2-binary or psycopg2
  nacl → PyNaCl
  jwt → PyJWT
  jose → python-jose
  toml → toml or tomllib (built-in ≥3.11)
  dateutil → python-dateutil
  attr, attrs → attrs
  google.cloud.* → google-cloud-*
  azure.* → azure-*
test files: test_*.py, *_test.py, tests/ → dev/test usage

Config-referenced tools — these packages appear in config files, NOT in import statements:
  pytest-* plugins → pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] addopts/plugins, pytest.ini, setup.cfg [tool:pytest]
  flake8-* plugins → .flake8, setup.cfg [flake8]
  mypy extensions → mypy.ini, pyproject.toml [tool.mypy] plugins
  pylint-* → .pylintrc
  coverage, pytest-cov → .coveragerc, pyproject.toml [tool.coverage]
  black, isort, autopep8, ruff → pyproject.toml [tool.black/isort/ruff]
  pre-commit hooks → .pre-commit-config.yaml
  sphinx-* → docs/conf.py extensions list

Django/Flask framework packages:
  INSTALLED_APPS in settings.py counts as usage for django-* packages
  Flask extensions registered via app.register_blueprint() or init_app() count as used
CLI tools declared in [project.scripts] or [tool.poetry.scripts] are used even without import statements
missing_dep_rule: Note: PyPI name and import name may differ; apply name mapping above
